meaning: n.微弱的闪光,一丝光线,瞬息的一现vi.闪烁,隐约地闪现vt.使发微光,使闪烁
examples: I saw the gleam of the knife as it flashed through the air.
There is no gleam of hope.
Their hope did not last long; the gleam was quickly eclipsed.
Today, the Cape York project is a gleam in the eye of a private Australian developer.
No wonder the Passat, and the Volkswagen plant that manufactures it, has attracted gleam-filled gaze from the north.
